data.title:
Aurelius Technology proposes two-for-one bonus issue with free one-for-one warrants
Electronics manufacturing services provider Aurelius Technologies Bhd (KL:ATECH) is planning a bonus share issue on the basis of two new shares for every existing share held, along with the issuance of bonus warrants on the basis of one free warrant for every share held.
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (April 15): Electronics manufacturing services provider Aurelius Technologies Bhd (KL:ATECH) is planning a bonus share issue on the basis of two new shares for every existing share held, along with the issuance of bonus warrants on the basis of one free warrant for every share held.

The proposed bonus issuances will involve up to 867.46 million new Aurelius shares, and up to 433.73 million free warrants. The entitlement dates will be fixed and announced later, according to the group’s bourse filing on Tuesday.

As at April 3, Aurelius had an issued share capital of RM391.06 million comprising 433.52 million shares.

For illustrative purposes, based on the five-day volume weighted average market price of Aurelius shares of RM3.1571 on April 3, the theoretical ex-bonus share price will be about RM1.0524.

Meanwhile, the exercise price of warrants is assumed to be RM1.07 each, representing a premium of 1.68% to the theoretical ex-bonus price of RM1.0524. Based on this exercise price, and assuming full exercise, Aurelius expects to raise gross proceeds of RM463.87 million, which will be allocated for its working capital and funding for future investment.

In June 2024, Aurelius completed a private placement exercise involving up to 39.41 million new shares — representing not more than 10% of the total number of issued shares — raising gross proceeds of about RM132.01 million.

For the 12 months ended Dec 31, 2024 (FY2024), Aurelius’ net profit doubled to RM61.02 million from RM29.57 million a year ago, while its total revenue rose 128% to RM604.17 million from RM264.99 million in FY2023.

Shares of Aurelius were down four sen or 1.5% at RM2.71 at Tuesday’s midday break, giving it a market capitalisation of RM1.175 billion. Year-to-date, the stock has fallen 22.6%.

Edited ByIsabelle Francis

data.title:
MSB Global eyes trade war benefits from China sourcing despite weak ACE Market debut
Autopart distributor MSB Global Group Bhd (KL: MSB) sees potential gains from US-China trade tensions through lower import costs, even as it marked a weak debut on Bursa Malaysia's ACE Market — becoming the sixth straight listing since March to fall on its first trading day.
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (April 15): Autopart distributor MSB Global Group Bhd (KL:MSB) sees potential gains from US-China trade tensions through lower import costs, even as it marked a weak debut on Bursa Malaysia's ACE Market — becoming the sixth straight listing since March to fall on its first trading day.

Executive director Datuk Ow Chen Lun attributed the muted listing to broader market uncertainties but highlighted trade war advantages to the company, which sources parts and components primarily from China.

"The tariff from the US may cause goods to be redirected to Malaysia from China and result in lower purchasing costs for us.

"This in turn will generate higher margins. We cannot provide a forecast result, but we are optimistic about this opportunity," he said at Tuesday's press conference.

The listing provides us with a platform to move forward with our long-term strategic plans,” he added while remaining optimistic about long-term growth through lubricant investments and EV segment expansion.

MSB Global distributes GSP-branded automotive components in Malaysia, including driveshafts, wheel hub assemblies, suspension parts and steering racks.

The company’s long-standing 23-year relationship with its suppliers is expected to help mitigate potential cost fluctuations, should they occur.

From the RM41.4 million raised through its public issue, the group plans to allocate 22.58% to the purchase of new machinery and equipment, 18.7% for constructing a new factory and warehouse, and 3.14% toward the development of an in-house EV charger — marking its early steps into the EV ecosystem.

Additionally, 20.67% of the proceeds will be used to repay bank borrowings with the remainder earmarked for working capital and listing-related expenses.

A concurrent private placement raised RM14.8 million for key shareholders including managing director Datuk Ow Kee Foo, executive director Lai Swee Ping and Lee Li Lian, a related party to one of MSB Global’s subsidiaries.

The group does not have a dividend policy and will declare dividends accordingly when the cashflow and profit of the company permit, said MSB Global executive director Datuk Ow Chen Lun after a press conference following the listing.

Looking ahead, MSB Global is setting its sights on Southeast Asia for potential regional expansion. Ow reaffirmed that while the domestic market remains the primary focus, any future growth strategy will concentrate on neighbouring markets within the region.

At the time of writing, MSB Global shares were 2.5 sen or 12.5% lower at 17.5 sen on Tuesday, valuing the group at RM107 million.

Also read:
Autopart distributor MSB Global slips 15% on ACE Market debut

Edited ByIsabelle Francis

data.title:
PGF Capital to see weaker 4Q q-o-q amid seasonal factors and market pressures — TA Securities
Penang-based insulation products manufacturer PGF Capital Bhd (KL:PFG) is expected to report a quarter-on-quarter (q-o-q) decline in earnings for the upcoming quarter, largely due to seasonal factors and ongoing capacity constraints.
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (April 15): Penang-based insulation products manufacturer PGF Capital Bhd (KL:PFG) is expected to report a quarter-on-quarter (q-o-q) decline in earnings for the upcoming quarter, largely due to seasonal factors and ongoing capacity constraints.

In a note on Tuesday, TA Securities said year-on-year (y-o-y), performance is projected to remain subdued, citing limited production capacity as a key factor hindering earnings growth on an annual basis.

"We expect the quarterly profit to range between RM3 million and RM6 million, representing a respective y-o-y growth of between 107% and 129%," the research firm said.

The firm also expects the company to declare the usual final dividend, estimated at 1 to 1.5 sen per share.

The research house maintained a positive outlook on PGF Capital’s full-year performance, projecting the group to deliver over 100% growth on a cumulative basis, primarily due to the absence of start-up losses that had impacted results in the first half of FY2024 from its newly operational warehouses.

Meanwhile, in terms of pricing, the selling price of glass wool — PGF’s core product — is expected to stay elevated at RM6.00 per kilogram in the fourth quarter.

However, external risks remain as the company management noted that if the current 145% reciprocal tariff on Chinese glass wool persists, excess supply from China could eventually be redirected to Southeast Asian markets, which PGF has 20% exposure to.

On a more positive note, the company’s largest market, Australia — accounting for around 70% of PGF’s sales — is expected to remain resilient due to the unique product specifications and quality requirements mandated by Australian builders, which make substitution more difficult.

Broader market conditions have also weighed on PGF’s share price as the firm said the company has seen a 21% year-to-date decline, mirroring the overall downturn in the Malaysian equity market, where the FBMKLCI index has dropped 9.8% so far this year. TA Securities attributes this to profit-taking activities by retail investors following PGF’s strong performance in 2024.

Despite the short-term headwinds, TA Securities maintained its “buy” recommendation on PGF Capital, maintaining a target price of RM3.00 and made no changes to its FY25-27 earnings projections.

The group is involved in the manufacturing and distributing of glass mineral wool insulation and property development. PGF was formerly known as Poly Glass Fibre (M) Bhd.

At the time of writing, PGF shares were down one sen or 0.6% at RM1.70 on Tuesday, valuing the group at RM330 million.

Edited ByIsabelle Francis

data.title:
Kerjaya Prospek bags RM98 mil contract for works at Andaman Island in related-party deal
Kerjaya Prospek Group Bhd (KL:KERJAYA) has secured a building contract valued at RM98.09 million for works to be carried out at Bandar Tanjung Pinang, Andaman Island.
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (April 15): Kerjaya Prospek Group Bhd (KL:KERJAYA) has secured a building contract valued at RM98.09 million for works to be carried out at Bandar Tanjung Pinang, Andaman Island.

The contract was awarded to its wholly owned subsidiary Kerjaya Prospek (M) Sdn Bhd by Persada Mentari Sdn Bhd, an indirect subsidiary of Eastern & Oriental Bhd (KL:E&O).

In a bourse filing on Tuesday, the project encompasses the execution and completion of two phases of building works — Phase 1 will involve the development of 22 three-storey terrace houses and eight three-storey semi-detached homes, while Phase 2 will comprise 28 three-storey terrace houses and 18 three-storey semi-detached homes.

The contract is classified as a related party transaction, as Kerjaya Prospek’s non-independent non-executive chairman Datuk Seri Tee Eng Ho also serves as the executive chairman of E&O. He is a major shareholder of both companies.

Work on the contract is scheduled to commence on April 22, 2025, and is expected to be completed within 24 months from the commencement date.

Kerjaya Prospek said the contract is expected to contribute an additional revenue stream to the group over the next two financial years.

While it will have no impact on the issued share capital of the company, the group said the contract is anticipated to contribute positively to the group’s earnings and net assets per share for the financial years ending 2025 through 2027.

Shares of Kerjaya Prospek rose two sen or 1% to RM2.05 on Tuesday, valuing the group at RM2.58 billion. Year-to-date, the stock fell 7.24%.

Edited ByIsabelle Francis

data.title:
Mid-cap stocks doled out higher dividends in FY2024
THE financial year ended 2024 was a great year for investors, as many Malaysian corporates paid out higher dividends, as well as special dividends, proving that not just big-cap blue chips can afford to give generous dividends.   Many agree that the higher dividends are largely att...
data.description:
This article first appeared in The Edge Malaysia Weekly on April 7, 2025 - April 13, 2025

THE financial year ended 2024 was a great year for investors, as many Malaysian corporates paid out higher dividends, as well as special dividends, proving that not just big-cap blue chips can afford to give generous dividends.  

Many agree that the higher dividends are largely attributed to the 2% tax on individuals’ dividend income exceeding RM100,000 effective Jan 1, 2025. 

Yet there were companies that declared special dividends in the October-to-December quarterly earnings announcements that came after the effective date of the 2% dividend tax. 

For companies with a market capitalisation of some RM920 million to RM2.8 billion listed on Bursa Malaysia, 36 declared higher dividends in FY2024. 

Twenty-one declared lower dividends, 16 had the same payout as in the previous year while 27 did not pay dividends for the financial year.

(For data on companies with a market cap of above RM2.8 billion, see our cover story titled “Fatter dividends declared in FY2024 ahead of 2% dividend tax”, in Issue 1567, March 24, 2025.) 

In this category of companies, British American Tobacco (M) Bhd (KL:BAT) paid out the highest dividend per share (DPS) of 59 sen for the financial year ended Dec 31, 2024. This is the tobacco firm’s lowest DPS over the last three financial years.

While the payout remains above 90%, it is worth noting that the dividends dished out on a per share basis have been declining over the last three financial years, from 88 sen per share in FY2022 to 63 sen in FY2023 and 59 sen in FY2024.

BAT’s earnings per share (EPS) has been shrinking as well, falling from 91.9 sen in FY2022 to 68.2 sen in FY2023 and 64.1 sen in FY2024.  

While its EPS in FY2024 was lower at 64.1 sen, revenue rose a marginal 0.19% to RM2.32 billion from RM2.31 billion in FY2023. The lower earnings were attributed to reduced margins from its vapour products and a slight contraction in market share for its combustible products, but were within the company’s expectations.  

Apart from BAT, six other companies declared a DPS of above 20 sen in FY2024. These were Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd ­(KL:DLADY) with a dividend payout of 50 sen per share, MBM Resources Bhd (KL:MBMR) with 45 sen, Ta Ann Holdings Bhd (KL:TAANN) with 35 sen, Bermaz Auto Bhd (KL:BAUTO) with 26 sen, Malaysia Smelting Corp Bhd (KL:MSC) with 31 sen and Uchi Technologies Bhd (KL:UCHITEC) with 29.5 sen. 

Dutch Lady’s dividend payout has been consistent over the last four financial years, at 50 sen per share. The amount per share is, however, lower than the pre-pandemic levels of RM1 or more.  

MBM Resources, BAuto and MSC all paid special dividends during FY2024. MBM Resources paid the highest special dividends in FY2024 at 32 sen per share, bringing the total DPS for the year to 45 sen. Notably, the auto assembler pays special dividends yearly.  

BAuto, which has an April 30 financial year end, rewarded shareholders with a special dividend of seven sen per share in FY2024. The auto manufacturer, which has been giving out special dividends annually in the month of June, appears to have pushed forward the dividend in its most recent financial year. The announcement for the special dividend for FY2025 was made at the beginning of last December. 

The company’s three largest shareholders are Dynamic Milestone Sdn Bhd (15.1%), the Employees Provident Fund (13.69%) and Lembaga Tabung Haji (6.57%). Dynamic Milestone is linked to BAuto’s executive chairman Tan Sri Yeoh Choon San and group CEO Datuk Lee Kok Chuan as well as Datuk Amer Hamzah Ahmad. 

Investors of BAuto should be pleased given that the DPS and payout ratio grew over the three financial years in review — from 8.75 sen (payout ratio of 65.54%) in FY2022, to 22 sen (83.68%) in FY2023 and 26 sen (86.44%) in FY2024. This is in tandem with the growth in its earnings, which has risen from 13.4 sen per share in FY2022 to 30.1 sen in FY2024. 

Meanwhile, MSC announced a special dividend of 17 sen per share for FY2024, which was paid on Dec 23, 2024. Including the interim and final dividends of seven sen each, the mining and smelting company’s DPS came up to a hefty 31 sen for FY2024, compared with 14 sen in FY2023 and seven sen in FY2022. 

Based on its EPS of 18.9 sen for FY2024, MSC’s payout ratio comes up to 164%, which far exceeds the company’s dividend policy, which targets a payout ratio of at least 30% of net profit attributable to owners of the company. 

DPS growing over last three years

Notably, the DPS of a number of companies has been on a rising trend over the three financial years in review. 

One of the companies is Crescendo Corp Bhd (KL:CRESNDO), which has seen its DPS grow from four sen in FY2022 to five sen in FY2023 and 18 sen in FY2024. (The company’s financial year end is Jan 31.)  

This commensurates with the property developer’s earnings, which have grown significantly, particularly in FY2024 — driven by sales of industrial plots to data centre operators — when compared with FY2023. Net profit increased to RM57.06 million (or 20.3 sen per share) in FY2024 from RM24.53 million (or 8.8 sen per share) in FY2023. 

However, in Crescendo’s most recent results announcement, DPS for FY2025 declined to 10 sen, according to its financial statement for the fourth quarter ended Jan 31, 2025 (4QFY2025). It should be highlighted that Crescendo underwent a share split exercise, where every one ordinary share was split into three. The exercise was completed last September.  

Shareholders of Kerjaya Prospek Group Bhd (KL:KERJAYA) have also seen its DPS grow over the last three financial years. The construction company’s dividend payout increased from six sen in FY2022 to eight sen in FY2023. It paid a substantial 15 sen per share in FY2024, pushing its dividend payout ratio to 118%. 

Of the DPS of 15 sen for FY2024, four sen were paid out as a special dividend in December.

The dividend yield of Kerjaya Prospek’s shares is hovering above 5% and at the close of trading on April 4, its share price was RM2. Over the period of one year, the company’s share price has increased by 20.48%.  

The major shareholder of Kerjaya Prospek is Datuk Seri Tee Eng Ho, who holds a 67.64% stake. Tee is also the non-independent non-executive chairman of the group. In addition, he serves as executive chairman of Eastern & Oriental Bhd (KL:E&O), where he is also the major shareholder with a 58.26% stake. 

A back-of-the-envelope calculation shows Tee received at estimated RM127.8 million in dividends from Kerjaya Prospek in FY2024.  

 

Save by subscribing to us for your print and/or digital copy.

P/S: The Edge is also available on Apple's App Store and Android's Google Play.

data.title:
Stocks with momentum on April 15, 2025: Magma, HPP Holdings
theedgemalaysia.com highlighted two stocks on momentum at Bursa Malaysia’s noon break on Tuesday. Both stocks displayed negative momentum.
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (April 15): theedgemalaysia.com highlighted two stocks on momentum at Bursa Malaysia’s noon break on Tuesday. Both stocks displayed negative momentum.

The stocks with negative momentum were:

  • Magma Group Bhd (KL:MAGMA) — up 2.5 sen or 10% to 27.5 sen
  • HPP Holdings Bhd (KL:HPPHB) — up two sen or 4.12% to 50.5 sen

The list of stocks with momentum is generated using a proprietary mathematical algorithm highlighting stocks with a build-up in trading volume and price. The algorithm differentiates between stocks that exhibit positive (+ve) momentum and negative (-ve) momentum.

This list is not a buy or sell recommendation. It merely tells you which stocks are seeing higher-than-normal volume and price movements.

The share price may move up or down from this point. But the “+ve” (suggesting a rising price trend on volume) and “-ve” (suggesting a falling price trend on volume) indicators should give readers a better idea of what the market is buying and when to sell. Note also that momentum generally only persists for a short period of time.

However, each stock has an accompanying fundamental score and valuation score to help readers evaluate the attractiveness of the stocks if they want to ride the momentum.

For more detailed financial information and reports on the above-mentioned stocks, please subscribe to AbsolutelyStocks at www.absolutelystocks.com

Edited ByJason Ng

data.title:
Zarul Ahmad denies holding grudge, says he's indebted to Lim Guan Eng for Penang undersea tunnel project
Datuk Zarul Ahmad Mohd Zulkifli, a former director of Consortium Zenith BUCG Sdn Bhd, told the sessions court he was grateful to Lim Guan Eng for helping secure the Penang undersea tunnel project and denied holding any grudge against him.
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (April 15): Datuk Zarul Ahmad Mohd Zulkifli, a former director of Consortium Zenith BUCG Sdn Bhd, told the sessions court he was grateful to Lim Guan Eng for helping secure the Penang undersea tunnel project and denied holding any grudge against him.

Zarul Ahmad denied claims by Lim’s defence that he held a grudge against the former Penang chief minister and gave testimony implicating the politician out of spite.

Replying to questions from DPP Datuk Wan Shaharuddin Wan Laden in re-examination, he denied having any revengeful feelings against Lim.

“In fact, I am indebted to him as I received the project and the land (Lot 702) without submitting a complete feasibility study and detailed design on the tunnel.

“My company only submitted a feasibility study and detailed design (FSDD) over the three proposed roads and not the tunnel, and in return we got Lot 702 and 713 when submitting the request for proposal. Hence, I am indebted to Lim,” he said.

Zarul Ahmad added that the FSDD for the tunnel is still not completed.

On Monday, it was revealed that Lot 702 was held in booking for late businessman Datuk Ewe Swee Kheng and the luxurious City of Dreams twin tower project.

Ewe passed away in 2021, and his recorded statement to the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission was used in the trial.

Wan Shaharuddin: Who assisted you to receive the benefit ?

Zarul Ahmad: Lim Guan Eng.

Yesterday, the witness testified that Ewe paid RM12 million in 2013 to “book” Lot 702, just before the project was awarded to his company. In total, Zarul Ahmad’s company received RM130 million for the project.

Zarul said his company had a distinct advantage during the Request for Proposal (RFP) stage because it was the only one aware of a fourth part of the project — a back road from Tanjung Bungah to Teluk Bahang — which other bidders did not know about.

He added that during a project briefing, his firm gained another edge, as Lim Guan Eng had earlier promised to award them the project through direct negotiation. When it later shifted to an open tender, Lim allegedly assured Zarul that his company would still win the contract.

The trial will continue on May 14 before Sessions Judge Azura Alwi. Lim’s lawyers, Haijan Omar and RSN Rayer, informed the court that Lim plans to request the return of his passport next month for a trip to China.

Judge Azura instructed them to submit a formal application for the request.

Proceedings were cut short on Tuesday to allow Lim to pay his last respects to former Prime Minister Tun Abdullah Ahmad Badawi who passed away yesterday evening.

Lim, who is Bagan MP and Ayer Putih assemblyman, is on trial for using his position as the then-Penang chief minister to solicit a 10% cut in the RM6.3 billion undersea tunnel project’s profits from Zarul Ahmad, in return for aiding his company to secure the project.

The 64-year-old former finance minister is also accused of accepting RM3.3 million in kickbacks. A purported RM2 million paid on Aug 18, 2017, via businessman G Gnanaraja, is said to be part of the RM3.3 million.

DAP advisor Lim also faces two counts of dishonest misappropriation of property in releasing two plots of state-owned land, cumulatively worth RM208.75 million, to Ewein Zenith Sdn Bhd and Zenith Urban Development — two property companies linked to the controversial undersea tunnel project.

Read also:
Zarul Ahmad now says his firm received a distinct advantage from Guan Eng in the Penang undersea tunnel project

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title:
Insider Moves: Ecofirst Consolidated Bhd, XL Holdings Bhd, Turiya Bhd, KIP Real Estate Investment Trust, YTL Power International Bhd, YTL Corp Bhd, Sunway Bhd
Notable filings During the week of March 24 to 28, notable shareholding changes at Bursa Malaysia-listed companies included those at property developer Ecofirst Consolidated Bhd (KL:ECOFIRS), which saw Morgan Stanley & Co International plc cease to be a substantia...
data.description:
This article first appeared in Capital, The Edge Malaysia Weekly on April 7, 2025 - April 13, 2025

Notable filings

During the week of March 24 to 28, notable shareholding changes at Bursa Malaysia-listed companies included those at property developer Ecofirst Consolidated Bhd (KL:ECOFIRS), which saw Morgan Stanley & Co International plc cease to be a substantial shareholder. According to filings with Bursa, the global financial services firm disposed of 57.01 million shares or 4.8% equity interest on March 21.

Morgan Stanley emerged as a substantial shareholder of Ecofirst on Jan 22 after acquiring 59.83 million shares or a 5.06% stake. It went on to acquire small blocks of shares until end-February, when it had amassed 69.28 million shares or 5.86% shareholding in Ecofirst.

It is not clear who Morgan Stanley was holding the block of shares for. At press time, it was not known who had acquired Morgan Stanley’s block of shares in Ecofirst.

Ecofirst shares have been trading at between 36 sen and 39 sen since the beginning of the year. The share price closed at 38 sen last Wednesday, giving the company a market capitalisation of RM446.4 million.

Filings with Bursa during the week in review also showed that Morgan Stanley ceased to be a substantial shareholder of XL Holdings Bhd (KL:XL), formerly known as Xian Leng Holdings Bhd. Fish farming, edible bird nests and pineapple farming, among others, are the company’s mainstay.

Morgan Stanley ceased to be a substantial shareholder of XL after selling 4.4 million shares on March 19, reducing its stake to below the 5% threshold required for public disclosure. It emerged as a substantial shareholder of XL on Nov 8 last year after acquiring 21.95 million shares or 5.04% shareholding.

Until March 11 this year, Morgan Stanley was nibbling at XL’s stock and held 26.46 million shares or 5.99% of XL before the selldown. XL’s share price closed at 52 sen last Wednesday, giving the company a market value of RM230.1 million.

At Turiya Bhd (KL:TURIYA), Tan Sri Dr Mohan M K Swami ceased to be a substantial shareholder of the electroplating and property investment outfit after his private vehicle Khidmat Kejora Sdn Bhd sold 66.08 million shares or 28.89% equity interest to Singaporean outfit Bin Zayed (S) Pte Ltd, which is linked to the Bin Zayed International Group of companies. Bin Zayed was founded by Sheikh Khaled Zayed Saquer Zayed Alnahyan, a member of the UAE royal family. Businessman Datuk Seri Shamir Kumar Nandy is executive director at Bin Zahid (S).

Mohan had been involved in Turiya since 1994, when it was known as Sitt Tatt Bhd, but briefly exited the company when it was taken over by MAA Group Bhd (KL:MAA) in June 2021. He made a comeback in August last year, taking control of the company after a legal dispute with MAA Group.

Turiya’s share price closed at 30 sen last Wednesday, giving the company a market capitalisation of RM68.6 million.

Over at KIP Real Estate Investment Trust (KL:KIPREIT), Hextar Rubber Sdn Bhd ceased to be a substantial shareholder during the week in review after selling 32.17 million units in the REIT.

Hextar Rubber had emerged as a substantial shareholder in KIP REIT in September 2021 after acquiring 50 million units or a 9.89% stake in the REIT, which is involved in retail, industrial and commercial properties.

In October 2022, Hextar Rubber held 120.86 million shares or a 20.87% stake in KIP REIT. In July 2024, it began selling its units in the REIT.

KIP REIT hit a record low of 86 sen per unit in intraday trading on March 12. The units closed at 87 sen apiece last Wednesday, giving the REIT a market value of RM694.8 million.

Notable movements

YTL Power International Bhd’s (KL:YTLPOWR) share price had shed about 25%, or RM1.14, since early January to close at RM3.37 last Wednesday. This gave the company a market capitalisation of RM27.7 billion.

The Employees Provident Fund (EPF) re-emerged as a substantial shareholder of the utility player in mid-January, with 414.27 million shares or a 5.04% stake. In March, the provident fund started mopping up YTL Power’s shares on the open market. During the week in review, it bought 15.61 million shares, increasing its shareholding in the company to 510 million shares or 6.2% equity interest.

EPF was also accumulating shares in YTL Power’s parent company YTL Corp Bhd (KL:YTL), which holds a 54.86% stake in the utility firm. During the week in review, the provident fund acquired 17.45 million shares in YTL Corp, nudging its shareholding to 639.39 million shares or 5.79% equity interest.

EPF was also trading the shares of Malaysian conglomerate Sunway Bhd (KL:SUNWAY) during the week in review, accumulating 6.17 million shares to take its shareholding to 520.97 million shares or 8.43% equity interest. In early February, EPF held 498.88 million shares or an 8.08% stake in the company.

Sunway’s share price had gained 9.7% since mid-January to close at RM4.50 last Wednesday, valuing the group at RM27.8 billion. 

 

Save by subscribing to us for your print and/or digital copy.

P/S: The Edge is also available on Apple's App Store and Android's Google Play.

data.title:
Malaysian semiconductor stocks fall amid US probes, software firms steady
Malaysia's technology sector turned lower on Tuesday as the US announced investigations into imports of semiconductors, setting the stage for the Trump administration to impose tariffs.
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (April 15): Malaysia's technology sector turned lower on Tuesday as the US announced investigations into imports of semiconductors, setting the stage for the Trump administration to impose tariffs.

The Bursa Malaysia Technology Index, which tracks 49 stocks in the sector, fell 2% by noon trading break. Inari Amertron Bhd (KL:INARI), the sector’s biggest firm by market capitalisation, was down nearly 4% while Frontken Corporation Bhd (KL:FRONTKN) and ViTrox Corporation Bhd (KL:VITROX) both fell about 3%.

Software and information technology companies, meanwhile, appeared unperturbed as MyEG Services Bhd (KL:MYEG), ITMAX System Bhd (KL:ITMAX), and CTOS Digital Bhd (KL:CTOS) held steady.

The US is investigating imports of semiconductors and pharmaceuticals under the so-called Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962 on grounds that extensive reliance on foreign production of medicine and chips is a national security threat.

Section 232 probes must be completed within nine months of initiation. The investigation, which began on April 1, follows exemptions announced over the weekend for consumer electronics and semiconductor equipment from the punitive reciprocal tariffs.

Global tech stocks have staged a relief rally on Monday even as analysts and the industry cautioned that the Trump administration may target semiconductor imports separately from the sweeping tariffs announced on April 2.

Read also:
US steps up probes into pharmaceutical, chip imports, setting stage for tariffs
Malaysia's semicon firms brace for future US action after dodging tariffs

Edited ByJason Ng

data.title:
Nike set to build out new Shanghai studio in China push
Nike Inc is opening a new creative studio in Shanghai as the world’s largest sportswear company pumps investment into one of its most crucial markets.
data.description:
(April 15): Nike Inc is opening a new creative studio in Shanghai as the world’s largest sportswear company pumps investment into one of its most crucial markets.

The in-house production division, called Icon Studios, will create content including digital videos, product photography and projects across e-commerce, social media and live streaming, according to new job listings. 

Nike has been adding new facilities in China in recent years, including a technology centre in Shenzhen and a sports research lab in Shanghai. The company is looking to get closer to Chinese shoppers and recapture in a market that accounted for US$7.5 billion (RM33.1 billion) in annual revenue in fiscal 2024 — or 15% of global sales. That’s down from fiscal 2021, when sales of nearly US$8.3 billion represented 19% of the total. 

New chief executive officer Elliott Hill visited the country late last year shortly after coming out of retirement to take the top job. 

Nike shares are down 27% so far this year, amid concerns about the impact of US tariffs under President Donald Trump. 

“We continue to be excited about China for the long term,” Hill told investors in December. He added that the company is “making products of China, for China”, and referred to the product-development research lab in the country. 

Hill said in March that Shanghai is one of Nike’s five key cities — alongside Beijing, New York, Los Angeles and London. Nike has another Icon department in Los Angeles, which produces marketing imagery and video for the company’s products. 

Former Apple Inc marketing executive Jason King is leading Icon for Nike in Shanghai. The management plans to add about two dozen employees to the group, with roles in production, video editing, business planning, operations and logistics, according to the listings.

The in-house production studio will deliver “‘China-right’ creative content at speed and scale”, according to the listings. 

A representative of Nike declined to comment.

Read also:
China suppliers mock tariffs with Nike, Lululemon deals on TikTok

Uploaded by Tham Yek Lee

data.title:
Maybank customers can now make QR payments through MAE app in Cambodia
Malayan Banking Bhd (KL:MAYBANK) announced on Tuesday that Maybank customers travelling to Cambodia can make instant KhmerQR retail payments via the MAE mobile app at a low conversion fee.
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (April 15): Malayan Banking Bhd (KL:MAYBANK) announced on Tuesday that Maybank customers travelling to Cambodia can make instant KhmerQR retail payments via the MAE mobile app at a low conversion fee.

There are currently nine million MAE app users.

The initiative is on the back of the second phase of cross-border QR payment linkage between Cambodia and Malaysia implemented by Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) and the National Bank of Cambodia in Kuala Lumpur on April 8.

“This initiative underscores our commitment to enhancing financial accessibility and mobility across the region, ultimately driving a more connected and dynamic marketplace economy,” said president and group chief executive officer Datuk Khairussaleh Ramli.

Cambodia is the latest country added to its list of international markets including Singapore, Indonesia, Thailand and China where the MAE app can be used to make QR transactions.

Southeast Asia’s fourth largest bank by assets, Maybank, reported an 83% year-on-year growth in total value of transactions made via QR payments in 2024.

In the statement, Maybank shared its commitment in strengthening cross-border ties through financial empowerment and playing an active role as Malaysia assumes this year’s Asean chairmanship.

“As one of the largest providers of retail QR payments in Malaysia, we are committed to continuously enhancing convenience, security and accessibility for our customers,” said Khairussaleh.

Maybank also supported the initial phase of the launch in September 2024, which enabled Cambodian travellers to conveniently make DuitNow QR retail payments via the Bakong App in Malaysia.

Read also:
Malaysia, Cambodia launch QR linkage for instant cross-border payments
Malaysians can now use their mobile payment apps for transactions in Cambodia

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title:
Eco-Shop says to launch Main Market IPO in 'coming weeks'
Eco-Shop Marketing Bhd said on Tuesday it will soon launch its initial public offering (IPO) on the Main Market after signing an underwriting agreement with Maybank Investment Bank.
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (April 15): Eco-Shop Marketing Bhd said on Tuesday it will soon launch its initial public offering (IPO) on the Main Market after signing an underwriting agreement with Maybank Investment Bank.

The dollar store retailer plans to release its prospectus “in the coming weeks”, Eco-Shop CEO Jessica Ng said in a statement. Under the agreement, Maybank will underwrite the retail portion of the IPO.

“The IPO will not only enhance our financial position but also strengthen our ability to capture the vast opportunities in Malaysia’s thriving value retail market,” Ng said.

Eco-Shop operates 358 stores nationwide, selling over 10,000 third-party and house-brand products ranging from toys to toothpaste. Most of its products are priced at RM2.40 while the rest are below RM20.

Backed by Creador Capital Group, the private equity firm that also invested in Mr DIY Group (M) Bhd (KL:MRDIY), Eco-Shop was reported by Bloomberg in March 2023 to be seeking to raise up to RM800 million from the listing.

Eco-Shop is seeking to expand, particularly in suburban and rural areas, and open about 70 new stores per year over the next five years, Ng said.

The listing will provide investors with “unique exposure to Malaysia's value retail segment”, said Michael Oh-Lau, CEO of Maybank Investment Bank. Eco-Shop would be “a compelling proposition for investors”, he said.

The proposed IPO involves an institutional offering of up to 675.368 million shares and a retail offering of 186.778 million shares, according to its draft prospectus filed with the Securities Commission Malaysia.

Eco-Shop is presently controlled by its founder and managing director Datuk Seri Lee Kar Whatt with a directly owned 80.3% stake and an indirect 2% stake, while Creador holds a 10% stake. Both are offering up shares for sale in the IPO.

Post-IPO, Lee's direct stake will drop to 73.9% while Creador will be left with 1.9%.

Maybank Investment Bank is the principal adviser and sole underwriter for the IPO. It is also the joint global coordinator and joint bookrunner of the IPO, together with UBS. RHB Investment Bank is also a joint bookrunner.

Read also:
Creador-backed discount store retailer Eco-Shop eyes Main Market listing, plans to float 862 mil shares

Edited ByJason Ng

data.title:
TNB bags RM705 mil maintenance contract from Kuwait’s Ministry of Electricity, Water and Renewable Energy
Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) (KL:TENAGA), via its wholly owned subsidiary TNB Repair and Maintenance Sdn Bhd (TNB REMACO), has secured a RM705 million contract from the Kuwait Ministry of Electricity, Water, and Renewable Energy for maintenance works at the Sabiya Power Generation and Water Distillation Plant in Kuwait.
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (April 15): Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB) (KL:TENAGA), via its wholly owned subsidiary TNB Repair and Maintenance Sdn Bhd (TNB REMACO), has secured a RM705 million contract from the Kuwait Ministry of Electricity, Water, and Renewable Energy for maintenance works at the Sabiya Power Generation and Water Distillation Plant in Kuwait.

The seven-year contract, awarded through a joint venture with Kuwait-based Al Dhow Engineering, will cover maintenance, repair, and overhaul services for the facility located 65km from Kuwait City.

The plant houses four oil- and gas-powered units with a total generation capacity of 299.4 megawatts, playing a key role in Kuwait’s national energy supply.

TNB president and chief executive officer Datuk Megat Jalaluddin Megat Hassan said the agreement demonstrates confidence in the joint venture’s capabilities.

“This contract aligns with TNB’s mission to be a leading provider of sustainable energy solutions, both in Malaysia and internationally. Beyond Kuwait, our global footprint extends to the United Kingdom, Ireland, Australia, Türkiye, Saudi Arabia, Pakistan, and Cambodia,” Megat Jalaluddin said in the statement.

The signing ceremony for the contract was held on March 12, 2025 at the National Bank of Kuwait Head Office. It was officiated by Megat Jalaluddin and Al Dhow Engineering chairman Isam Al-Sager. Also present were TNB Power Generation Sdn Bhd (TNB Genco) managing director and TNB REMACO chairman Datuk Muhamad Nazri Pazil and TNB REMACO managing director Abidin Sarjo.

Muhamad Nazri said TNB REMACO has built a strong presence in Kuwait since entering the market in 2013.

He said the company now manages 11 active projects worth over RM4.54 billion. Since 2013, 16 TNB experts have been sent to Kuwait, with six currently managing the ongoing projects.

TNB REMACO, a subsidiary of TNB Genco, specialises in operations, maintenance, and engineering services for gas, steam, and hydro turbines, as well as boilers and generators across global markets.

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title:
Malaysia's prolonged policy opacity weighs on investment, margins and sentiment — PublicInvest Research
The Malaysian government’s ongoing policy uncertainty is dampening investment, compressing margins and affecting market sentiment, said PublicInvest Research.
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (April 15): The Malaysian government’s ongoing policy uncertainty is dampening investment, compressing margins and affecting market sentiment, said PublicInvest Research.

"The government’s strategic engagement aims to preserve sectoral competitiveness and secure carve-outs via its role in global value chains.

"However, prolonged policy opacity is already weighing on investment, margins and sentiment," said the research firm as it cut its 2025 GDP forecast to 4.2%.

Further, it added, Malaysia’s ability to adapt policies with credibility will be crucial — not just for maintaining investor trust but also for positioning the country as a stable hub in an increasingly fragmented global economy.

"While the ringgit faced depreciation pressure immediately following the tariff announcement, it has since stabilised, supported by the temporary exemptions on key exports and the 90-day enforcement pause. This has alleviated some near-term currency volatility, although broader uncertainty remains," said the house in a note on Tuesday.

It maintained their USD/MYR forecast at 4.45 to 4.55, supported by narrowing rate differentials, fiscal discipline and contained monetary policy.

The firm expects Bank Negara to maintain the overnight policy rate at 3% throughout the year, emphasising the need for preserving macroeconomic stability in the face of global trade uncertainties.

“With growth momentum expected to moderate and inflation risks largely contained under the targeted subsidy framework, there appears to be limited justification for either tightening or easing,” said the research house.

The current stance allows policymakers to be strategic while navigating a hostile trade environment without adding procyclical pressure.

PublicInvest Research cut their 2025 gross domestic product forecast down to 4.2%, previously 4.9%, due to trade disruptions and delayed investments in the manufacturing and electronics sector.

“We believe the deeper issue lies in the breakdown of forward visibility for firms operating across integrated supply chains,” said analysts.

Despite the temporary 90-day pause on US reciprocal tariffs, the lack of transparency of policies clouds the outlook in the months ahead. Malaysia’s response to the tariffs is to negotiate, not retaliate, which could further weigh heavily on investments, margins and sentiment, according to the research note.

PublicInvest Research said these uncertainties could be offset by domestic demand as household consumption is still moderate due to the implementation of minimum wage hikes. They added that ongoing initiatives such as digital infrastructure development and public-private collaboration in high-value sectors lend medium-term support to the economy.

In the research note, their consumer price index forecast remained at 2.4%, in support of the Ron95 fuel subsidy as they expect the targeted nature of the policy would keep inflation risks contained amidst price-dampening effects from weak global demand.

PublicInvest Research noted that Malaysia needs to focus on policy flexibility and credibility to maintain investor trust and stay relevant in global supply chains amidst an unstable world economy.

Edited ByIsabelle Francis

data.title:
Trump floats temporary reprieve for autos as parts tariffs loom
President Donald Trump said he is exploring possible temporary exemptions to his tariffs on imported vehicles and parts to give auto companies more time to set up US manufacturing.
data.description:
(April 15): President Donald Trump said he is exploring possible temporary exemptions to his tariffs on imported vehicles and parts to give auto companies more time to set up US manufacturing.

“I’m looking at something to help car companies with it,” Trump told reporters Monday in the Oval Office. “They’re switching to parts that were made in Canada, Mexico and other places, and they need a little bit of time because they’re going to make them here.”

The president was asked what short-lived product exclusions he was considering but did not specify how long a potential pause or lowering of auto levies would remain in place.

The president’s comments could potentially bring relief to automakers reeling from his duties on car and light truck imports but they also inject further uncertainty into his tariff plans. Shares of General Motors Co, Ford Motor Co and Chrysler parent Stellantis NV hit session highs after Trump’s comments, reversing earlier declines.

Levies on auto imports have threatened to raise prices for American consumers and wreak havoc on auto supply chains which are deeply integrated across the US, Canada and Mexico. Trump has argued the duties are necessary to revive American manufacturing.

Trump imposed a 25% tariff on fully built vehicles with duties on parts set to take effect no later than May 3. His separate tariffs on Canada and Mexico already contain a carve-out for vehicles with enough domestic content to meet requirements under the existing North American trade agreement.

Detroit’s Big Three automakers for weeks have lobbied the Trump administration to exclude certain low-cost car components from planned tariffs. Ford, GM and Stellantis have conceded they’re willing to pay tariffs on completed cars and large components like engines and transmissions, according to people familiar with the matter.

“There has to be a runway of some kind to make the changes the administration would like to see,” said Mitch Zajac, an automotive and supply chain attorney with Butzel Long in Detroit. “If we have to do this, it can’t be done overnight.”

Company representatives have told the administration that broad parts duties would drive up costs and trigger profit warnings and layoffs that would counter Trump’s goal of rebuilding US auto manufacturing. Carmakers are expected to shoulder much of the tariff burden, at least initially, with margins at many parts manufacturers already running thin.

Tariffs could add as much as US$20,000 (RM88,110) to the cost of some imported luxury vehicles, according to a report this month from research firm Anderson Economic Group. Even at the low end of the market, small sedans and crossovers with high US content could incur US$2,500 to US$4,500 in additional costs.

The group’s report estimated US consumer impact at US$30 billion for the first full year. That would have a substantial impact on affordability, with buyers already facing average new-car prices approaching US$50,000.

Pharmaceutical imports

The US president also said Monday he anticipated rolling out tariffs on pharmaceutical imports in the “not too distant future”.

Trump’s frequent shifts on his sweeping tariff plans have sent confusing signals to markets, companies and trading partners grappling with how to engage a White House pledging to negotiate dozens of new agreements aimed at shrinking trade imbalances.

The president on Monday touted exclusions for popular consumer electronics from 125% tariffs on China and a 10% baseline global tariff as beneficial for American tech giant Apple Inc and also touted US investments by AI chipmaker Nvidia Corp.

“Look, I’m a very flexible person, I don’t change my mind, but I’m flexible,” Trump told reporters.

“I helped Tim Cook recently and that whole business,” the president continued, referring to the Apple CEO. “I don’t want to hurt anybody. But the end result is we’re going to get to the position of greatness for our country.”

Trump signalled over the weekend, however, that relief for tech products will be short-lived, with him and his aides saying that those imports will eventually be hit with a different, sector-specific tariff.

Uploaded by Arion Yeow

data.title:
Autopart distributor MSB Global slips 15% on ACE Market debut
Autopart distributor MSB Global Group Bhd (KL:MSB) fell 15% in its trading debut on Tuesday, the latest in a series of first-day flops on the ACE Market.
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (April 15): Autopart distributor MSB Global Group Bhd (KL:MSB) fell 15% in its trading debut on Tuesday, the latest in a series of first-day flops on the ACE Market.

MSB Global opened at 17 sen versus its initial public offering (IPO) price of 20 sen per share. The stock slipped to as low as 16.5 sen before paring the loss to 18 sen by 9.05am after close to 14 million shares changed hands. At the last price, MSB Global has a market capitalisation of RM104 million.

The drop comes even as the broader market continues to recover from the US tariff turmoil. If current prices hold, MSB Global would be the sixth consecutive listing since March to drop on their first day of trading on the ACE Market.

Investor demand during its IPO, which raised RM41.4 million, was also relatively weak with applications from retail investors totalling about six times the available shares for subscription.

MSB Global is a distributor of GSP-branded automotive parts and components in Malaysia, including driveshafts, wheel hub assemblies, suspension parts and steering racks.

The company is allocating 22.58% of the total proceeds from the public issue to buy new machinery and equipment, about 18.7% for the construction of a new factory and warehouse, and another 3.14% for its new in-house electric vehicle charger.

MSB Global will also set aside 20.67% for the repayment of bank borrowings, while the balance is for general working capital and to defray the listing expenses.

An offering of existing shares through private placement, meanwhile, raised RM14.8 million that went entirely to managing director Datuk Ow Kee Foo, executive director Lai Swee Ping, and Lee Li Lian, the wife of a director in one of MSB Global’s subsidiaries.

M&A Securities is the principal adviser, sponsor, sole underwriter and placement agent for the IPO.

Read also:
Auto parts distributor MSB Global's IPO sees 6.46 times oversubscription
Autopart distributor MSB Global sets ACE Market IPO price at 20 sen per share
Autopart distributor MSB Global gets approval for ACE Market IPO
Johor-based autopart distributor MSB Global files for ACE Market IPO

Edited ByJason Ng

data.title:
HRD Corp's chief executive Shahul Hameed steps down, confirming The Edge report
Datuk Shahul Hameed Dawood has stepped down as Human Resources Development Corp’s (HRD Corp) chief executive after five years at its helm, confirming a report by The Edge Malaysia weekly.
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (April 15): Datuk Shahul Hameed Dawood has stepped down as Human Resources Development Corp’s (HRD Corp) chief executive after five years at its helm, confirming a report by The Edge Malaysia weekly.

In a Facebook post, HRD Corp said Shahul is taking his "final bow" as chief executive effective Tuesday.

Meanwhile, there is no mention of the reason behind Shahul's departure or who will be replacing him.

He was appointed to the top job at HRD Corp in April 2020.

HRD Corp is expected to issue an official statement on Shahul's departure later today.

Earlier this month, The Edge Malaysia reported that Shahul was leaving HRD Corp, which comes under the purview of the Ministry of Human Resources.

HRD Corp is responsible for collecting levies from 800,000 employers for staff training and upskilling purposes.

Shahul and HRD Corp were thrust into the spotlight in July last year, following findings from the Public Accounts Committee and the Auditor General’s Report 2024 that exposed alleged irregularities that happened before 2022 at the agency.

Current Human Resources Minister Steven Sim Chee Keong, who was appointed in December 2023, also ordered a report to be lodged with the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission immediately after the allegations surfaced.

Read also:
Shahul Hameed set to leave HRD Corp, say sources

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title:
US steps up probes into pharmaceutical, chip imports, setting stage for tariffs
The Trump administration is proceeding with probes into imports of pharmaceuticals and semiconductors as part of a bid to impose tariffs on both sectors on grounds that extensive reliance on foreign production of medicine and chips is a national security threat, Federal Register filings on Monday showed.
data.description:
(April 14): The Trump administration is proceeding with probes into imports of pharmaceuticals and semiconductors as part of a bid to impose tariffs on both sectors on grounds that extensive reliance on foreign production of medicine and chips is a national security threat, Federal Register filings on Monday showed.

The filings announce 21-day public comment periods and mark President Donald Trump's latest use of Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962 as justification for so-called sectoral tariffs aimed at boosting domestic production of goods he says are critical to national security.

Using the 232 provision, the Trump administration has started investigations into imports of copper and lumber, and probes completed during Trump's first term formed the basis for 25% tariffs rolled out since his return to the White House in January on steel and aluminum and on the auto industry.

The filings, which indicate the administration began the investigations on April 1, follow exclusions unveiled over the weekend for smartphones, computers and other electronics imported largely from China from Trump's steep 125% reciprocal duties. Trump officials had said those products would soon be subject to Section 232 tariffs.

Section 232 probes need to be completed within 270 days of their initiation.

Trump has made use of tariffs a central plank of his administration's economic and national security policies, rolling out a series of aggressive levies against trading partners that economists estimate have lifted the average import duty to around 25% from just 2.5% in matter of months.

The announcements have roiled financial markets, with most US stock indexes now down 10% or more from record highs hit following Trump's election win in November. Waves of economists have also downgraded their outlooks for the American economy, many foretelling higher joblessness and inflation in the wake of Trump's tariffs.

A top Federal Reserve official — Governor Christopher Waller — earlier on Monday called Trump's tariff policy "one of the biggest shocks to affect the US economy in many decades."

'Breathing room and visibility'

The US began collecting baseline tariffs of 10% on most US imports on April 5, and Trump on April 9 put on hold even stiffer levies aimed at goods at dozens of other trading partners, although the heftiest tariffs targeting China remain in place. Pharmaceuticals and semiconductors are exempt from those duties, but Trump has said they will face separate tariffs.

Trump said on Sunday he would be announcing tariffs on imported semiconductors over the next week, adding that there would be flexibility with some companies in the sector.

The US relies heavily on chips imported from Taiwan, something former President Joe Biden sought to reverse during his term by granting billions of dollars in Chips Act awards to lure chipmakers to expand production in the US.

The notices published on Monday showed the investigations will include both pharmaceuticals and pharmaceutical ingredients as well as other derivative products.

Drugmakers have argued that tariffs could increase the chance of shortages and reduce access for patients. Still, Trump has pushed for the fees, arguing that the U.S. needs more drug manufacturing so it does not have to rely on other countries for its supply of medicines.

Companies in the industry have lobbied Trump to phase in tariffs on imported pharmaceutical products in hopes of reducing the sting from the charges and to allow time to shift manufacturing.

Large drugmakers have global manufacturing footprints, mainly in the US, Europe and Asia, and moving more production to the US involves a major commitment of resources and could take years.

"This notice gives us some breathing room and visibility on when the tariffs might be expected, and we will be certainly looking out for the lobby actions that PHRMA and the industry CEOs will be engaging in over the next three weeks," said Bernstein analyst Courtney Breen.

"We are bracing for announcement of tariffs around mid-May and see tariffs of 10%-25% as being possible, with the industry angling for a slow ramping to these tariffs and potential carve-outs," Breen said.

Gary Shapiro, CEO and vice chair of the Consumer Technology Association, said that Trump's Friday exclusion on smartphones, computers and semiconductors recognized that his tariffs will hurt consumers.

"At the same time, the shift from IEEPA to Section 232 as a legal basis for tariffs reveals the Administration’s desire for a more durable justification," Shapiro said in a statement. "But claiming that downstream consumer tech products qualify as 'semiconductors' is a stretch."

He called for "a smarter, targeted trade strategy where we team up with allies to compete with China."

Uploaded by Jason Ng

data.title:
Asian stocks set for cautious day as US trade probes sow more anxiety
Asian stocks are poised for a cautious opening after White House plans to impose tariffs on semiconductor and pharmaceutical imports pushed US futures down.
data.description:
(April 15): Asian stocks are poised for a cautious opening after White House plans to impose tariffs on semiconductor and pharmaceutical imports pushed US futures down.

S&P 500 futures fell 0.4% in early trading after the index gained almost 1% on Monday as President Donald Trump’s administration signaled a reprieve on consumer electronic tariffs. Treasuries snapped a five-day slide that drove 10-year yields up by the most in over two decades. Stock futures in Tokyo, Hong Kong and Sydney pointed to modest gains on Tuesday.

Investors are still struggling to game out the economic spillovers of the trade war given the back-and-forth in negotiations. While US officials insist the tariff strategy is carefully constructed, critics see the trading order as subject to the whims of a transactional president.

The White House on Monday announced trade probes into semiconductors and pharmaceuticals, seen as a precursor to imposing tariffs that threaten to broaden Trump’s sweeping US trade war. They could take months to play out.

Meantime, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent played down the recent selloff in the bond market, rejecting speculation that foreign nations were dumping their holdings of US Treasuries, while flagging that his department has tools to address dislocation if needed.

“I don’t think there’s a dumping” by foreign investors, Bessent said in an interview Monday with Bloomberg Television while on a visit to Buenos Aires, Argentina. He pointed to what he said was increased foreign demand at auctions for 10-year and 30-year Treasury securities last week.

The biggest Wall Street firms have highlighted how difficult it has been to predict the trajectory for equities. Barclays plc’s Venu Krishna said the recent volatility leaves little confidence in any pricing now, while JPMorgan Chase & Co’s Dubravko Lakos-Bujas indicated that forecasting in the current environment is a challenge and leaves a wide range of outcomes.

“As we begin this week, again, traders will be fixated to social-media feeds and the newswires for the latest on this never ending saga of ‘tariffs-on, tariffs-off’,” said Jay Woods at Freedom Capital Markets. “One thing the current administration has been great at is keeping market participants on their toes.”

Strategists at BlackRock Inc’s research arm said they are dialing up their risk-taking and embracing US and Japanese stocks following the Trump administration’s pause of tariffs on many global trading partners, even as they steer clear of longer-dated US debt.

“The near-term risk of a financial accident has eased,” wrote the BlackRock Investment Institute strategists including Jean Boivin and Wei Li. “Checks on policy allowed us to extend our tactical horizon back to six to 12 months and resume our positive view on US and Japanese stocks.”

Japan’s super-long government bond yields surged on Monday on speculation that authorities will order an extra budget draft in response to tariffs. The 20-year yield soared seven basis points to 2.435%, its highest level since 2004, while the 30-year rate jumped 12 basis points to 2.845%.

Eyes will also be on Chinese President Xi Jinping’s first overseas trip of the year, after he landed in Vietnam on Monday, with visits to Malaysia and Cambodia also scheduled. He’s expected to present his nation as a more stable partner than the US under Trump.

Policymakers are closely monitoring a variety of similar gauges to assess whether Trump’s sweeping tariffs will result in more persistent price growth. While many economists forecast the duties to boost inflation — at least in the near-term — tariff threats so far haven’t changed consumers’ views over the long term.

Fed Governor Christopher Waller laid out two scenarios for how Trump’s trade policy could affect the US economy, but said the inflationary impact of either would likely be temporary.

Waller called the new tariff policy “one of the biggest shocks to affect the US economy in many decades,” in remarks prepared for an event in St Louis on Monday. Should there be a small tariff effect on inflation, rate cuts would “very much” be on the table for the latter half of 2025, he said.

Some of the main moves in markets:

Stocks

  • Hang Seng futures rose 0.8% as of 7.52am Tokyo time
  • S&P/ASX 200 futures rose 0.2%
  • Nikkei 225 futures rose 0.4%
  • S&P 500 futures fell 0.4%

Currencies

  • The Bloomberg Dollar Spot Index was little changed
  • The euro was little changed at US$1.1357
  • The Japanese yen was little changed at 143.10 per dollar

Cryptocurrencies

  • Bitcoin fell 0.3% to US$84,606.57
  • Ether fell 0.9% to US$1,619.38

Bonds

  • The yield on 10-year Treasuries declined 12 basis points to 4.37%
  • Australia’s 10-year yield fell four basis points to 4.36%

Commodities

  • Spot gold was little changed

Uploaded by Jason Ng

data.title:
South Korea unveils US$23 bil support package for chips amid US tariff uncertainty
South Korea announced on Tuesday an increase in its support package for the country's vital semiconductor industry to 33 trillion won (US$23.25 billion or RM102.60 billion), up about a quarter from a 26 trillion won package unveiled last year.
data.description:
SEOUL (April 15): South Korea announced on Tuesday an increase in its support package for the country's vital semiconductor industry to 33 trillion won (US$23.25 billion or RM102.60 billion), up about a quarter from a 26 trillion won package unveiled last year.

The measures come in response to calls on the government to expand support at a time of growing policy uncertainty under the current US administration and rising competition from Chinese rivals, the government said in a statement.

Seoul will also ramp up a financial assistance programme for the chips industry to 20 trillion won, versus the previous 17 trillion won, according to the joint statement from various ministries including the trade ministry.

South Korea's decision to pump more money into its key chips sector is designed to help companies cope with heavier costs as they compete globally, it said.

Asia's fourth-largest economy is home to the world's top memory chip makers, Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix, though they have fallen behind some rivals in areas such as chip design and contract chip manufacturing.

In 2024, South Korea's exports of semiconductors stood at US$141.9 billion, accounting for 21% of the country's total, government data showed.

Shipments to China and the US stood at US$46.6 billion and US$10.7 billion, respectively.

US President Donald Trump said on Sunday he would be announcing the tariff rate on imported semiconductors over the next week, adding that there would be flexibility with some companies in the sector.

Last week, South Korea announced emergency support measures for its auto sector, seeking to reduce the blow of US tariffs on a sector that has seen years of sharply rising exports to the US.

The measures include financial support for the auto industry as well as tax cuts and subsidies to boost domestic demand, while the government also vowed efforts to negotiate with the US and help expand markets.

Uploaded by Jason Ng
